metadata.title: Global Active Conflict Zones & Terrorism Hotspots Database
metadata.purpose: Research reference — conflict zones relevant to terrorism analysis
metadata.disclaimer: Approximate data based on open-source intelligence. Boundaries, population estimates, and intensity ratings are indicative, not authoritative. Conditions change rapidly.

conflict_zones.notes: HTS-administered zone; post-2024 political transition has altered dynamics significantly. Former rebel enclave now integrated into broader Syrian transitional governance discussions.
ISIS sleeper cells continue attacks. Turkish cross-border operations ongoing. Al-Hol and Roj displacement camps hold tens of thousands of ISIS-affiliated detainees.
Key ISIS resurgence corridor along the Euphrates Valley. Cross-border movement with Iraq. Tribal tensions between Arab population and Kurdish-led SDF.
ISIS maintains desert hideouts and conducts intermittent ambushes and IED attacks. Iraqi security forces conduct regular clearance operations.
Hamrin Mountains and rural Diyala remain ISIS stronghold areas. Ethno-sectarian tensions between Arabs, Kurds, and Turkmen compound security challenges.

active_groups: Al-Qaeda in the Indian Subcontinent (AQIS) elements
conflict_zones.notes: ISKP remains the primary threat, conducting attacks against Taliban, civilians, and foreign targets. This is ISKP's historical stronghold despite Taliban counter-operations.

active_groups: Various Central Asian jihadist groups (IMU remnants, Jamaat Ansarullah)
conflict_zones.notes: ISKP has expanded recruitment among ethnic Uzbeks and Tajiks. Cross-border threat to Central Asian states. Taliban conducts regular counter-ISKP operations.
Taliban heartland; relatively controlled but periodic ISKP attacks. Key narcotics production area fueling instability economics.
ISKP targets Shia Hazara communities, Taliban officials, and symbolic targets. High-profile attack location.
Houthi Red Sea/Gulf of Aden shipping attacks since late 2023 have drawn significant US/UK military strikes. Severe humanitarian crisis. Ceasefire efforts ongoing but fragile.
AQAP maintains operational capability in remote areas. Power vacuum between STC and government forces enables militant activity. Al-Bayda is key AQAP/ISIS battleground.

Al-Shabaab remains one of the most capable jihadist groups globally. Generates significant revenue through taxation/extortion. Government offensive since 2022 made gains but Al-Shabaab retains rural control.

active_groups: Boko Haram (Jama'atu Ahlis Sunna Lidda'awati wal-Jihad - JAS)
conflict_zones.notes: ISWAP is now the dominant faction after Boko Haram's Shekau died in 2021. Over 2 million IDPs. Lake Chad islands serve as key ISWAP base. Multinational Joint Task Force operations ongoing.
Banditry-terrorism nexus is growing. Mass kidnappings for ransom. Ansaru expanding influence among bandit groups. Some groups pledging allegiance to ISWAP. Mining areas particularly affected.

active_groups: Islamic State Sahel Province (ISSP, formerly ISGS)
Various self-defense militias (Dan Na Ambassagou, Koglweogo, etc.)
conflict_zones.notes: Epicenter of global jihadist expansion. JNIM and ISSP compete and clash. Post-coup military juntas in all three countries expelled French/UN forces. Wagner/Africa Corps mercenaries deployed in Mali and Burkina Faso. Massive displacement crisis.
Malian army recaptured Kidal from Tuareg separatists in late 2023 with Wagner support. JNIM and ISSP both active across the north. Tuareg resistance continues.

active_groups: Volunteers for the Defense of the Fatherland (VDP - government militia)
conflict_zones.notes: Over 40% of territory under jihadist influence or control. Estimated 2+ million IDPs. Numerous siege conditions in northern towns like Djibo. One of the fastest-deteriorating security situations globally.

Rwandan military intervention since 2021 significantly degraded insurgent capability. Insurgents displaced southward and into Tanzania/Nampula. LNG project (TotalEnergies) remains suspended. Over 1 million displaced.

M23 advanced significantly in 2024-2025, at times reaching the outskirts of Goma. ADF (ISIS affiliate) conducts mass-casualty attacks in Beni area. Over 120 armed groups active. 7+ million IDPs in eastern DRC. MONUSCO withdrawal accelerating.

Arakan Army has taken control of large parts of Rakhine since late 2023 offensive. Rohingya civilian population caught between competing armed groups. Massive displacement into Bangladesh. Humanitarian access severely restricted.

Operation 1027 (Oct 2023) by the Three Brotherhood Alliance captured major towns. MNDAA took Kokang. TNLA expanded control. Military regime lost significant territory. Ceasefire negotiations ongoing but fragile.

KIA is one of the strongest ethnic armed organizations. Sagaing is the heartland of the resistance movement against the junta. Military uses airstrikes and artillery against civilian areas. Significant PDF operations.
Karenni resistance controls significant territory. Karen forces took Myawaddy (key border trade town with Thailand) in early 2024. Massive displacement to Thailand border.

BLA Majeed Brigade has escalated sophisticated attacks (suicide, complex). TTP also very active. Attacks target security forces, Chinese nationals/CPEC projects, and Hazara Shia community. Significant escalation in 2024-2025.

TTP is the primary threat. Massive surge in attacks since Afghan Taliban takeover in 2021. Cross-border safe havens in Afghanistan. Pakistani military operations Azm-e-Istehkam launched. Sectarian violence in Kurram district extremely severe.
Sindhi separatist groups have increased low-level attacks. Karachi remains a target for sectarian and political violence. Lower intensity than Balochistan/KP but emerging trend.

BARMM (Bangsamoro Autonomous Region) transition has reduced mainstream Moro insurgency. Remaining threat is from ISIS-linked splinter groups. ASG significantly degraded. Marawi rebuilt. Kidnap-for-ransom still a concern in Sulu seas.
Spillover from Nigerian Boko Haram/ISWAP insurgency. Cross-border raids and suicide attacks. Multinational Joint Task Force operates in the area. Civilian vigilante groups (CPDM) active.
Anglophone separatist crisis since 2017. School boycotts, ghost towns. Multiple fragmented armed groups. Both sides accused of atrocities. Over 700,000 displaced. No peace process making progress.

active_groups: Various militias aligned to GNU (Tripoli) or LNA (Benghazi)
conflict_zones.notes: Divided governance between GNU (west) and LNA (east). Relative calm but underlying tensions remain. Southern Libya is a transit hub for weapons, fighters, and migrants flowing into the Sahel. ISIS reduced but not eliminated.

War between SAF and RSF erupted April 2023. One of the world's worst humanitarian crises — 10+ million displaced, famine conditions. RSF controls most of Darfur and Khartoum. Ethnic cleansing documented in Darfur. Risk of state collapse. Foreign powers fueling conflict.

Fano insurgency erupted after government attempted to dissolve Amhara regional forces (post-Tigray war). State of emergency declared. Fano controls rural areas; ENDF holds cities. Significant civilian toll.
OLA active primarily in western and southern Oromia. Ethnic dimension to conflict. Government conducts counter-insurgency operations. Periodic reports of civilian massacres by both sides.

active_groups: Eritrean forces (still present in border areas)
conflict_zones.notes: Cessation of Hostilities Agreement (Nov 2022) ended main conflict. But Eritrean troops remain, Western Tigray contested. Humanitarian crisis continues. Disarmament proceeding slowly.

active_groups: FARC dissident factions (Estado Mayor Central - EMC, Segunda Marquetalia)
conflict_zones.notes: Peace process with EMC and ELN ongoing but fragile. FARC dissidents control coca-growing regions. Clashes between armed groups for territorial control. Venezuelan border region particularly volatile. Forced displacement continues.

active_groups: Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine (PFLP)
conflict_zones.notes: Major escalation since October 7, 2023 Hamas attack and subsequent Israeli military operations. Unprecedented civilian casualties and destruction. Majority of population displaced, severe famine conditions. Ceasefire negotiations ongoing. Catastrophic humanitarian situation.

Significant escalation since October 2023. IDF raids in Jenin, Tulkarm, Nablus intensified. Settler violence surged. New armed groups emerged. Palestinian Authority governance weakening. Settlement expansion accelerating.

active_groups: Various Sunni extremist cells (Ain al-Hilweh camp)
conflict_zones.notes: Major escalation in 2024 between Israel and Hezbollah, including Israeli ground incursion into southern Lebanon and extensive airstrikes on Beirut suburbs. Hezbollah senior leadership decimated. Ceasefire in late 2024. Massive displacement and destruction of southern towns.

conflict_zones.zone_name: Sahel Expansion - Coastal West Africa (Togo/Benin/Ghana borders)
conflict_zones.notes: Jihadist groups from Burkina Faso expanding southward into coastal West African states. Multiple attacks in northern Togo and Benin since 2021. WAP park complex is key infiltration corridor. Ghana's Upper East under increasing threat. Represents the frontline of Sahel conflict expansion toward the Gulf of Guinea coast.

Post-coup Niger (July 2023) expelled French and US military forces. ISSP extremely active in Tillabéri (Mali-Niger border). Boko Haram/ISWAP threatens Diffa (Lake Chad border). Security vacuum growing.
ISIS-Somalia maintains a small but persistent presence in the Bari highlands. Conducts periodic attacks and generates revenue through taxation of trade routes.
Lake Chad islands serve as base for Boko Haram/ISWAP fighters. Cross-border attacks affect all four Lake Chad Basin countries. Chadian military is the most capable in the region.

Egyptian military's comprehensive operation (Sinai 2018) significantly degraded ISIS-Sinai. Attacks greatly reduced but not eliminated. Bedouin population affected by displacement and restrictions. Gaza crisis creates new border tensions.
Significantly reduced threat from 2015-2016 peak. Small cells persist in mountainous Algerian border areas. Security forces maintain heavy presence.

Post Article 370 abrogation (2019), insurgent attacks reduced but continue. TRF emerged as new front for Pakistan-backed groups. Encounters in forested areas of Rajouri/Poonch increased. Cross-border infiltration continues.
Chin resistance forces control much of rural Chin State. Towns like Thantlang heavily destroyed. Displacement to India (Mizoram). Resistance linked with broader anti-junta movement.

active_groups: Coalition of Patriots for Change (CPC - remnants)
conflict_zones.notes: Wagner/Africa Corps mercenaries deployed since 2018 supporting government. Armed groups still control territory outside Bangui. Human rights abuses by all parties. Resource competition (diamonds, gold, cattle) drives conflict.

Worst atrocities of the Sudan war concentrated in Darfur. RSF and allied Arab militias accused of ethnic cleansing against Masalit, Fur, and Zaghawa. El-Fasher under prolonged siege. Echoes of 2003 Darfur genocide. Massive cross-border displacement to Chad.

active_groups: Wagner Group remnants / Russian volunteer battalions
conflict_zones.notes: Full-scale Russian invasion since February 2022. While primarily a conventional interstate war, included here due to terrorism-relevant dynamics: drone/missile attacks on civilian infrastructure, occupation zone resistance, and foreign fighter flows.
Jaish al-Adl conducts cross-border attacks from Pakistan. PJAK sporadically active in Kurdish regions. ISIS claimed major attack in Kerman (Jan 2024). Border security remains a challenge.
Al-Shabaab conducts cross-border attacks and IED ambushes targeting KDF and civilians. Boni Forest serves as operational base. Periodic attacks on infrastructure and communication towers.
Houthi drone and missile attacks on Saudi territory significantly reduced since Saudi-Houthi diplomatic engagement. Main Houthi threat shifted to Red Sea shipping. AQAP domestic plots disrupted.

active_groups: AQIM (Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb) remnants
Smuggling networks linked to jihadist groups
conflict_zones.notes: Algeria's security forces maintain strong control. Southern border region vulnerable to spillover from Sahel. AQIM leadership relocated south into Sahel. Algeria opposes foreign military intervention in neighboring states.

Insurgency largely suppressed under heavy security. ISIS-K claimed Crocus City Hall attack (Moscow, March 2024). Radicalization pipeline continues — many North Caucasus fighters joined ISIS in Syria/Iraq. Kadyrov's forces maintain tight control in Chechnya.

conflict_zones.zone_name: Bangladesh - Chittagong Hill Tracts / Rohingya camps
conflict_zones.notes: Nearly 1 million Rohingya refugees. ARSA maintains presence in camps. Drug trafficking (yaba) through Myanmar border. Radicalization risk in camps due to poor conditions and lack of opportunity. Repatriation stalled.
MIT leader Ali Kalora killed in 2021. Group severely weakened. Indonesia's Densus 88 counter-terrorism unit very effective. Remaining threat is from JAD cells and lone actors inspired by ISIS.
Low-level Malay Muslim insurgency since 2004. Peace talks with BRN have made some progress. Violence has decreased but IEDs, shootings, and arson continue. Not linked to global jihadist networks.

Colombian armed groups operate freely in Venezuelan border regions. ELN has significant presence in Apure and Zulia. Complex narco-political dynamics. Venezuelan government alternately cooperates with and confronts these groups.

Epicenter of Fulani-Dogon communal violence exploited by jihadists. Katiba Macina (JNIM) recruits heavily from Fulani communities. Mass atrocity events. Wagner operations have drawn allegations of civilian massacres (Moura, 2022).

Farmer-herder conflict with ethnic and religious dimensions. Frequent mass casualty attacks on villages. Some evidence of jihadist groups exploiting the violence. One of the most lethal conflict dynamics in West Africa by civilian death toll.

Gangs control ~80% of Port-au-Prince. While not terrorism per se, included due to armed non-state actor dynamics, mass displacement, and potential extremist exploitation of state collapse. Kenya-led MSS deployed but limited impact so far.

Included due to scale of violence (30,000+ homicides/year) and narco-terrorism dynamics. Sinaloa Cartel fractured after Zambada/Guzmán arrests. CJNG expanding aggressively. Chiapas emerging as new hotspot. Not traditional terrorism but terrorism-adjacent dynamics.

Subnational violence continues despite 2018 peace agreement. Delayed elections and transitional period. Flooding and famine exacerbate crisis. Spillover from Sudan war creating new refugee influx. Not terrorism-focused but terrorism-enabling environment.
Assassinations, IEDs, and ambushes continue. Captagon drug trade generates significant revenue. Druze community in Sweida periodically protests. ISIS conducts sporadic attacks from Badia desert.

conflict_zones.zone_name: Red Sea / Gulf of Aden - Maritime threat zone
conflict_zones.notes: Houthi anti-shipping campaign since November 2023 is unprecedented. Attacks using drones, ballistic missiles, and naval mines against commercial vessels. US/UK conducted strikes on Houthi positions. Major disruption to global trade. Shipping rerouted around Cape of Good Hope.

top_threat_groups_by_geographic_reach: ISIS (and provinces): Syria, Iraq, Afghanistan, Somalia, Mozambique, DRC, West Africa, Egypt, Libya, Caucasus, Southeast Asia
Al-Qaeda (and affiliates: JNIM, AQAP, AQIM, AQIS, HTS-origin): Yemen, Sahel, North Africa, Somalia, South Asia, Syria
Boko Haram / ISWAP: Nigeria, Cameroon, Chad, Niger
